subject: Anthropology
title: The Potlatch
description: A quick win: understand The Potlatch.

hook.text: A chief gives away everything he owns — blankets, canoes, food — and gains more status than if he kept it.

buildup.text: The potlatch is a gift-giving feast among Northwest Coast peoples where hosts gain prestige by giving away wealth.

discovery.text: The more you give, the higher your rank. Rivals compete by outdoing each other's generosity.

twist.text: Colonial governments banned potlatch, calling it wasteful. They couldn't see that giving was the whole point.

climax.text: Potlatch shows that wealth isn't universal. In some systems, you gain power by distributing, not hoarding.

punchline.text: In some worlds, giving is the greatest power.

quiz.question: How does a chief gain status in a potlatch?
options: By giving away the most wealth
